text: The attempts made to explain this verse as it stands are not such as to produce conviction, and it seems likely that there is some corruption or omission in the text.

‘He who guards the realm of Persia’; the reference here, and all through the rest of the chapter, is to the guardian angels of the countries concerned, according to the common opinion. The last clause in this verse is of very doubtful interpretation.

Literally, ‘As it were the semblance of a son of man touched my lips’. This would suggest, at first sight, the presence of a second angelic being, not yet mentioned. But verse 19 probably implies that only one angel is referred to, from verse 5 onwards.

Verses 20, 21 do not read naturally, and some think the order of the original text has become dislocated through an accident. ‘The book of doom’; literally, ‘the writing of faithfulness’ (i.e. certainty).
